01 The opportunity

Ticketing has three jobs
at a concours.

Win the guest

The whole journey is yours.

Branded event pages and checkout, styled to the show. Confirmations, tickets and calendar invites in your name. Not a marketplace listing wearing someone else's logo.

Sell through sponsors

Sponsors sell for you.

Co-branded pages, sales embedded on the partner's own site, scoped sponsor logins, comp codes tracked to the code.

Protect both

Nothing gets away from you.

Stripe-backed checkout, so card data never touches us. Race-safe holds keep a sold-out tier sold out. One live guest list.

02 What TicketAI is

One platform behind
every part of the event.

Describe the event in plain English. TicketAI builds the pages, the checkout, the codes and the automations, under your brand. Four views onto one system, in the order that follows.

View one · The guest journey

Invited, answered, reminded.

Every yes, free or paid → confirmation and calendar invite out, host alerted, counted against capacity.
That guest, from then on → reminded 7 days out, 1 day out, and the day itself. Nobody sends them.
View two · Running the event

Change it by asking.

"Add a Sunday VIP tier at $500 for 100 people" → live on the Sunday session, on the public page, in seconds.
Fourteen tools, one chat box → tiers, capacity, codes, checkout fields, emails. Every action logged.
View three · Sponsors & partners

Sell where the sponsor already is.

Availability embed → real inventory on the partner's own site.
PARTNER15 · every redemption → recorded against the order with the exact discount. The partner's sales are traceable to the code.
View four · Sales, payments & reporting

Money in. Numbers out.

Payment methods · the guest chooses → cards, wallets, Apple Pay, Google Pay, buy-now-pay-later and crypto, in any currency.
Revenue, tickets sold, top sellers → read live off the order ledger, or just ask the system in plain English.

03 View one · The guest journey

From first look
to the gate.

Free or paid. One night or a full weekend, consecutive or not. Here is the path a ticketed or RSVP guest takes.

Paid tickets Free RSVP Memberships Time slots
  • 1
    Discover. A branded event page at its own link, with the story, every experience and every tier.
  • 2
    Choose. Any level of complexity: tiers per day or per session, assigned seats or tables, add-ons like parking.
  • 3
    Buy or RSVP. Protected Stripe checkout. Card data never touches our servers, and the last ticket cannot be sold twice.
  • 4
    Confirm. A confirmation email, calendar invite, and a unique QR code and number per ticket, dated to the right session, not just the right day.
  • 5
    Arrive. Entry runs off a live list built from completed orders, with repeat RSVPs and plus-ones resolved to one row.
theticketai.com/e/niagara5000

Live today: a real branded event page and checkout, running on TicketAI.

Run it all from
one screen.

Every experience, tier, code, guest, and dollar in one live view, updating as sales happen.

  • ◆
    Multi-day & multi-session events, tiers, capacity & perks
  • ◆
    Guest & RSVP lists, check-in status, CSV export
  • ◆
    Real-time revenue, roles for the 7C team
  • ◆
    Or just ask: "Add a Sunday VIP tier at $500." Done.
  • ◆
    Anything else you need, ask for it in plain English. The dashboards are the floor, not the ceiling.

05 View three · Sponsor & partner integration

Partners as a
revenue channel.

The sponsor layer isn't an afterthought. It is built into the platform.

  • ◆
    Co-branded pages. Partner marques and venues, side by side with the event.
  • ◆
    Embeddable widget. Sell or RSVP directly on a sponsor's own website.
  • ◆
    Scoped sponsor logins. Each partner sees only their allocation and stats.
  • ◆
    Partner and press codes. Comp allocations tracked to the code.
  • ◆
    Sponsors can ask, too. Give a partner access and they can say "offer this as part of our package," and it is set up for them.
theticketai.com/e/…-preview

Live today: a real co-branded partner page (event × venue), on TicketAI.

06 View four · Sales, automation & reporting

It runs itself,
and it tells you everything.

  • ◆
    Confirmations, instantly. Every paid ticket emails the guest their ticket, QR code and calendar invite, and your team a confirmation. Every sale lands on the dashboard.
  • ◆
    Reminders, on your schedule. Two months out, a month out, 24 hours, two hours before. Ask for the schedule and every guest gets it. Nobody sends them.
  • ◆
    Updates on your schedule. Ask for daily, weekly or biweekly sales updates and they arrive by email, to whatever spec you set.
  • ◆
    Live feeds and reporting. Purchase alerts and webhooks into your tools, or straight into your CRM. Revenue, tickets sold and top sellers, read live off the order ledger, or just ask.
  • ◆
    Insights, on demand. Full details captured on every buyer, from contact through company and role. Ask who your top spenders and highest-value guests are, or who is most likely to buy, and get a straight answer.

07 For the Canadian Winter Concours

A proposed starting point.

Shaped to a concours, ready to shape further with your team as the program comes together.

For your guests

A concours-grade journey

Branded pages for the show, the parade, and hospitality: single tickets or the full weekend, styled to the marque.

For your sponsors

Partner packages built in

Co-branded pages for marques & venues, embeddable on partner sites, scoped access for each sponsor.

For your team

You run it, or we do

Drive it yourselves from one live view, or hand it to us: email us for a new code or a change and we set it up for you.

We'd love a short session with Natalie & Brooke to walk through it, map this to the event's tiers, sponsors and timeline, and show you live examples of it running.

08 Where to from here

Let's start with the
Concours, then keep going.

Ticketing first. When you're ready, the same team can take on the other two tracks you raised.

  • 1
    Now. A working session to shape Winter Concours ticketing.
  • 2
    Next. An agentic workflow database, a cleaner, role-aware alternative to Notion.
  • 3
    Then. Identity protection and automated takedown for the impersonation issue.
